ty was high, colonizers instead erected structures oriented toward extraction.  McCloskey (2015) locates the origins of the modern rich world in a transformation of ideas and i
ns, driving down mortality and raising material welfare (McCloskey 2015, p.8).  Mokyr (2005) refines the knowledge channel by documenting the institutional scaffolding of t
c strand of this stream locates the divergence earlier and in different forces. Pamuk (2007) argues that the Black Death reconfigured relative factor prices, and that risin
Malthusian trap through preventive checks (Pamuk 2007, p.6; Pamuk 2007, p.19). Broadberry et al. (2015) push the timing of divergence still further back for India, dating decline to t
to the Mughal period well before colonial rule (Broadberry et al. 2015, p.14).  Bryant (2006) qualifies these accounts by insisting on cumulative, path-dependent trajectorie
geographic one.  ---  ## References  1. Acemoglu, D., Johnson, S. and Robinson, J. (2001) 'The colonial origins of comparative development: An empirical investigation',
